commit.message:
perf: reduce API calls from 7 to 5 searches

- Combine PR comment searches into one
- Reuse search results for recommendations instead of extra queries
- Should avoid rate limit issues

feat: add personalized recommendations to metrics workflow

- Help-wanted suggestions: where to create issues based on expertise + low activity
- PRs needing review: matching CI/CD, docs, UI skills
- PR opportunities: recent issues in active repos needing PRs
- Focused on most active repos: docs, ui, ui-plugins
- Still simple plain text format

feat: add simple metrics tracker for clubanderson

- Runs every 6 hours + on-demand
- Tracks 3 metrics: help-wanted issues, PR comments, merged PRs
- Sends plain-text pass/fail email to andy@clubanderson.com
- Only 4 searches total (1 date + 3 metrics)

fix: clarify JSON output format to prevent parsing errors

- Add explicit instructions for safe-output JSON generation
- Warn about escaping quotes/special characters
- Emphasize outputting single valid JSON object
- Should prevent the 14 JSON parse errors seen in run 20042838349

fix: eliminate per-repo searches, reuse existing data

- Removed Section 4 per-repo search loops
- Now analyzes repo health from existing search results
- Reduces from 7+ searches to exactly 7 searches
- All searches are org-wide as intended

fix: complete workflow restructure to prevent loops

MAJOR CHANGES:
- Removed all numbered Step 1-11 structure (was confusing agent)
- Rewrote as single-pass task: collect data once, generate email, output JSON, stop
- Dates calculated ONCE at start, not repeatedly
- Removed 300 lines of repetitive step-by-step instructions
- Simple task-based prompt instead of procedural checklist
- Should eliminate looping behavior completely

fix: remove all other maintainers from list, keep only clubanderson

- Removed confusing reference to 18 other maintainers
- Clear single target: clubanderson only
- Eliminates any confusion about who to audit

fix: add hard stop rules and simplify to prevent loops

- Add numbered execution rules with explicit loop detection
- Simplify maintainer list (collapsed since locked to clubanderson)
- Add self-check: 'If you find yourself at Step 1 again, STOP IMMEDIATELY'
- Clarify mission: audit clubanderson once, output JSON, stop

fix: remove confusing sections causing workflow loops

- Remove 'Important Notes' section after example (was confusing agent)
- Add stronger LINEAR WORKFLOW warning at top
- Add clear termination marker at end of file
- Should prevent agent from restarting Step 1 within same run

fix: add explicit stop instructions to prevent intra-run loops

- Add 'STOP execution' after Step 10 safe-output creation
- Clarify mission: complete steps 1-11 exactly once
- Prevents agent from restarting analysis within same run

feat: add help-wanted issue creation suggestions to audit emails

- New Step 7: Analyze repo health + maintainer expertise
- Suggest specific areas where maintainer should create help-wanted issues
- Added new email section: 'Consider Creating Help-Wanted Issues'
- Includes what/where/why for each suggestion
- Helps grow contributor base by leveraging maintainer knowledge

fix: remove markdown headings from email template

- Replace ## headings with plain text labels (looks better in plain text emails)
- Remove backticks from repo names in examples
- Add explicit rule: DO NOT use markdown headings (##, ###)
- Email will now show '📊 **Quick Stats**' instead of '## 📊 Quick Stats'

fix: remove backticks from usernames in email markdown

- Add explicit formatting rules to avoid inline code backticks for @usernames
- Update example to clarify proper plain text formatting
- Email will now show '@clubanderson' instead of '`@clubanderson`'

fix: prevent loop in maintainer audit + lock to clubanderson for testing

- Add explicit 'ONCE' instructions to prevent repeated GitHub searches
- Add execution strategy section with clear linear workflow
- Lock workflow to always audit clubanderson (index 0) during testing
- Disable state updates while in test mode
- Should reduce from 147 turns to ~10-20 turns per run

fix: prevent loop in maintainer audit + lock to clubanderson for testing

- Add explicit 'ONCE' instructions to prevent repeated GitHub searches
- Add execution strategy section with clear linear workflow
- Lock workflow to always audit clubanderson (index 0) during testing
- Disable state updates while in test mode
- Should reduce from 147 turns to ~10-20 turns per run
Add User Acquisition section to maintainer metrics
Update Help-Wanted Suggestions to focus on creating new issues
fix: use correct total_count for merged PRs in metrics
Simplify metrics extraction to use total_count directly
Fix metrics extraction to use total_count directly
fix: Add debug output for merged PRs count
Fix metrics calculation and remove logo
Fix: Improve email formatting with proper indentation and HTML logo rendering
feat: add date to metrics email subject line
Add KubeStellar logo to email footer
Add explicit indentation requirement for email formatting
Add dynamic run-name to show maintainer ID in workflow title
fix: correct metric counting and add 8-space indentation for URLs
Use 5-space indentation for email recommendations
Update recommendation indentation to 8 spaces
debug: add explicit data display before metrics calculation

fix: clarify metric calculation instructions for agent

- Be more explicit about reading total_count field
- Emphasize deduplication for PR reviews count
- Should fix incorrect counts (2 vs 11, 59 vs 26)
fix: add pass/fail icons to all three metrics

refactor: reorder metrics to help-wanted, merged PRs, PR reviews

- Help-wanted issues now first in all sections
- Followed by merged PRs
- PR reviews/comments last
- Updated both metrics display and recommendations order

fix: send email to maintainer with CC to andy@clubanderson.com

- Added maintainer email mapping for all 19 maintainers
- Email now goes to the correct maintainer based on selection
- Andy receives CC copy of all audit emails
style: change recommendation indent from 3 to 5 spaces

feat: add wrapper workflow to run all 19 maintainers sequentially

- Created run-all-maintainer-audits.yml with matrix strategy
- Reverted clubanderson-metrics.md to single-input mode
- New workflow triggers audit for each maintainer using max-parallel: 1
- One click → 19 sequential audits

fix: remove stray 'done' statement causing syntax error

Removed leftover 'done' from removed for-loop

refactor: alphabetize maintainer list

All 19 maintainers now in A-Z order

feat: add 6 more maintainers to dropdown

Added:
- rxinui, vedansh-5, sagar2366, oksaumya, rupam-it

Total maintainers: 19

feat: add 7 more maintainers to dropdown

Added:
- nupurshivani, onkar717, kunal-511, mavrick-1
- gaurab-khanal, naman9271, btwshivam

Total maintainers: 14

feat: convert to workflow_dispatch with maintainer dropdown

- Removed cron schedule
- Added workflow_dispatch with choice input for all 7 maintainers
- Workflow now processes only the selected maintainer
- Enables manual per-maintainer audits

feat: add 5 more maintainers and change to weekly Monday schedule

Added maintainers:
- mikespreitzer (mspreitz@us.ibm.com)
- dumb0002 (Braulio.Dumba@ibm.com)
- waltforme (jun.duan@ibm.com)
- pdettori (dettori@us.ibm.com)
- francostellari (stellari@us.ibm.com)

Schedule: Mondays at 9am EST (14:00 UTC)
Format: Indented recommendation items (3 spaces)

fix: update email subject format

- Add rocket emoji 🚀
- Remove @ tick marks from username
- Add ✅/❌ for pass/fail status

fix: remove jq requirement - agent should parse JSON visually

Agent has permission issues with jq commands
Simplified instructions to just read files with cat and parse manually
The JSON data is small enough to read and understand

fix: clarify tool usage - only jq and bash allowed

Agent was trying to use python/node which aren't allowed
Updated prompt to explicitly use only jq for JSON processing
Delete .github/workflows/maintainer-audit.lock.yml

commit.message:
feat: add kproche and URL links to metrics tracker

- Fetches data for both clubanderson and kproche
- All recommendations include clickable GitHub URLs
- Sends separate emails to each maintainer

fix: add explicit URL requirements to recommendations

All recommendation items should include full GitHub URLs
Format: Title (repo #number) - https://github.com/...

feat: add maintainer-audit workflow for clubanderson and kproche

Sends audit reports to both maintainers every 6 hours with:
- Help-wanted issues created
- PR reviews/comments
- Merged PRs authored
- Personalized recommendations

fix: use --merged flag instead of --state merged

gh search prs --state only accepts open|closed, not merged
Use --merged boolean flag for merged PRs

fix: use --commenter instead of --involves for PR reviews

Matches the original GET_COMMENTED_MERGED_AND_OPEN_PR_COUNT function:
- Search merged PRs with --commenter (not --involves)
- Search open PRs with --commenter
- Combine unique PR numbers
Should now correctly show ~53+ PR reviews!

fix: clarify PR review calculation with explicit set difference

Make it crystal clear to subtract authored PRs from commented PRs
Should correctly show 150+ PR reviews now

fix: use --merged-at flag for date filtering

gh search prs --merged doesn't accept date parameters
Use --merged-at '>={date}' instead
This should correctly return 26+ merged PRs

fix: instruct agent to use send_email MCP tool

Agent should call send_email() MCP tool directly, not print JSON
This should finally make the email send!

fix: change safe-output type from send-email to send_email

Agent outputs send_email with underscore, not hyphen
This should fix the email delivery!

fix: clarify safe-output format and PR review calculation

- Agent must output raw JSON without markdown fences
- PR reviews = prs-commented MINUS prs-merged (exclude authored)
- This should fix both the email sending and incorrect counts

fix: add pagination and use --involves for PR reviews

- Add --limit flags to get all results (not just 30)
- Change --commenter to --involves to catch all PR interactions
- Should now correctly count 53+ PRs reviewed and 26+ merged
fix: correct bash tool syntax (patterns not allowed list)
fix: use closedAt instead of mergedAt field

fix: use gh search commands instead of gh api

Switched from gh api search/issues to proper gh search issues/prs
This should work correctly now

fix: correct gh api search syntax

Changed -q flag to -f q= for GitHub CLI search/issues endpoint
This should fix the HTTP 422 validation error

fix: redesign workflow with pre-fetched data

COMPLETE REDESIGN:
- New fetch-data job: Pre-downloads all 5 GitHub searches to JSON files
- Agent job: Only reads from files, NO GitHub search capability
- Agent CANNOT loop because it has no access to search tools
- Clean separation: data collection vs analysis
- Should eliminate looping behavior entirely

fix: completely restructure prompt to prevent loops

MAJOR REWRITE:
- Removed numbered step format (agent treats it as repeating checklist)
- Changed to narrative task description
- Emphasize 'ONE metrics email' and 'single-pass workflow'
- Clear flow: search → analyze → email → stop
- No more numbered steps that invite looping

fix: add explicit stop instructions to prevent search loops

CRITICAL FIX:
- Agent was looping and repeating the same 5 searches infinitely
- Added 'STOP searching' instruction after step 2
- Added warning: DO NOT calculate dates again, DO NOT repeat searches
- Clear progression: collect → analyze → email → stop

commit.message:
fix: Add label filter to technical-doc-writer workflow

Only run when issue has 'doc update' label to avoid unnecessary executions

fix: Increase create-issue max limit to 50

The default max:1 was preventing multiple issues from being created.
Agent found 5 PRs but could only create 1 issue due to the limit.

fix: Expose hours_lookback input as env variable HOURS_LOOKBACK

The agent needs the input as an environment variable to actually use it.
Added env section with: HOURS_LOOKBACK = github.event.inputs.hours_lookback || '1'

fix: Make hours_lookback parameter actually work in PR scanner

- Explicitly instruct agent to check github.event.inputs.hours_lookback
- Add clear step to determine lookback hours before searching
- Report actual lookback window in noop messages for verification

feat: Add hours_lookback input parameter to PR scanner

Allows custom lookback window when manually triggering workflow (default: 1 hour)

feat: Add workflow_dispatch to PR scanner and doc writer workflows

Allows manual triggering of both workflows from GitHub Actions UI

feat: Add automated PR scanning and doc update workflows

- Add scan-merged-prs workflow to hourly scan kubestellar org for merged PRs
- Add technical-doc-writer workflow to handle doc updates from PR changes
- Add technical-doc-writer agent profile customized for KubeStellar
- Workflows create issues with 'doc update' label and open doc PRs automatically

commit.message:
Fix test assertions: compare against HPA minReplicas instead of initial state

The test was failing when the deployment was already scaled from a
previous run. By comparing against the HPA's configured minReplicas
instead of the current deployment state, the test now correctly
verifies that the system recommends scaling up from the minimum
under load.

commit.message: Make load generator more aggressive: 10 workers, 500 req each, no wait between batches
Add labels to pod template so pods can be selected
Remove proactive model check - using capacity-based scaling
Re-enable cleanup step in CI workflow
Fix OOM: increase memory limits and reduce batch concurrency
Fix load gen script to ignore curl failures and exit cleanly
Add retry logic for vllm-service connectivity check

Update e2e tests: use direct vLLM service and parallel load jobs

- Patch HPA stabilization window to 2 minutes for faster e2e testing
- Replace heavy vllm-openai image with lightweight curl-based load generator
- Use vllm-service:8200 directly instead of gateway (avoids InferencePool routing issues)
- Create 5 parallel load generation jobs with 400 requests each to saturate vLLM

add OpenShift e2e CI workflow for self-hosted runner

- Add ci-e2e-openshift.yaml workflow that runs on self-hosted OpenShift runner
- Support in-cluster authentication for runner pods
- Remove KUBECONFIG requirement from Makefile and test suite

commit.message:
feat(helm): Add controller.enabled flag to skip controller deployment

Add controller.enabled parameter (default: true) to allow deploying
only VA/HPA/ServiceMonitor resources without a new controller instance.

Use case: Monitor additional variants using an existing cluster-wide
WVA controller without deploying duplicate controller instances.

Example:
  helm upgrade -i my-variant ./charts/workload-variant-autoscaler \
    --set controller.enabled=false \
    --set llmd.namespace=my-namespace \
    --set llmd.deploymentName=my-vllm-deployment

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Add upgrading section with CRD update instructions

- Document that Helm doesn't auto-update CRDs during upgrade
- Add instructions to manually apply CRDs before helm upgrade
- Note scaleTargetRef field addition as breaking change in v0.5.0
- Add command to verify CRD schema version on cluster

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(e2e): Add comprehensive test diagnostics and reliability improvements

Test Infrastructure:
- Add vLLM health check before load generation
- Add comprehensive diagnostics dump on HPA scaling failures
  (VA status, external metrics, HPA events, controller logs)
- Extend external metrics API timeout from 2 to 5 minutes
- Use release-specific label selectors for resource discovery
- Clean up existing load gen jobs before creating new ones

Load Generation:
- Use quay.io curl image to avoid Docker Hub rate limits
- Add arrival rate to HPA metric logging output
- Reduce HPA stabilization window to 30s for faster tests

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use scaleTargetRef for deployment lookup and fix metrics issues

Controller Fixes:
- Use scaleTargetRef.name for deployment lookup instead of VA name
- Use deployment name as key in saturation metrics maps
- Use deployment name in metric labels to match HPA selector
- Fix operator precedence bug in ConfigMap predicate
- Persist MetricsAvailable condition in saturation mode
- Make ConfigMap names configurable via environment variables

Metrics Fixes:
- Skip stale pod filtering when kube_pod_info returns empty
- Always update VA status with metrics even when no scaling decisions
- Filter services by release name label for multi-install support

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ci): Add ok-to-test gate, multi-install support, and PR isolation

CI Infrastructure:
- Add permission-based gate (admin/maintain/write can trigger e2e tests)
- Build WVA image from PR source before deploying
- Use cluster HF token secret instead of workflow secrets
- Add concurrency control to cancel previous runs on same PR
- Use PR-specific namespaces for test isolation:
  - llm-d-inference-scheduler-pr-$PR_NUMBER for llm-d infrastructure
  - llm-d-autoscaler-pr-$PR_NUMBER for WVA controller
- Label namespaces with openshift.io/user-monitoring for Prometheus
- Uninstall helm releases before deleting namespaces in cleanup

Helm Chart Multi-Install Support:
- Prefix all resources with release name for multi-install support
- Update HPA, VA, ServiceMonitor, vllm-service with release name
- Add variantCost field to VA template

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(test): set initialReplicas to 1 to match pod count assertion

Address Copilot review: the test expects HaveLen(1) but initialReplicas
was set to 2. Changed to 1 for consistency.

test(e2e): add accelerator-based VA grouping isolation test

Add e2e test to validate that VAs with the same model but different
accelerators are treated as separate scaling domains (issue #454).

The test:
- Creates two VAs with same model ID but different accelerators (A100, H100)
- Applies load only to the A100 deployment
- Verifies A100 VA scales up while H100 VA remains at minimum replicas
- This validates the accelerator-based isolation fix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(saturation): include accelerator type in VA grouping key

The saturation engine now groups VariantAutoscalings by model ID, namespace,
AND accelerator type. This ensures that the same model deployed on different
hardware (e.g., H100 vs L40) is treated as separate scaling domains.

Changes:
- Updated GroupVariantAutoscalingByModel() to include accelerator in key
- Added GetAcceleratorType() helper to extract accelerator from VA spec or label
- Added comprehensive unit tests for the new functionality

Fixes #454

commit.message:
feat(ci): post comment with link to e2e run on /ok-to-test

When a maintainer comments /ok-to-test on a fork PR, post a reply
comment with a direct link to the OpenShift E2E workflow run. This
makes it easy to find and monitor the e2e tests since issue_comment
triggered runs don't appear in the PR checks section.

commit.message:
fix: address Copilot review comments

- Use 'pending' status for skipped tests instead of 'success'
- Use github.workflow context variable for status context string

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): skip build-image for fork PRs on pull_request event

Fork PRs don't have access to repository secrets when triggered by
the pull_request event (GitHub security feature). This causes the
build-image job to fail with "Username and password required" even
when the job should run.

Changes:
- Add is_fork_pr output to gate job to detect fork PRs
- Skip build-image for fork PRs on pull_request event (they use
  issue_comment trigger via /ok-to-test instead)
- Add report-status job to update PR commit status after /ok-to-test
  triggered runs complete, so fork PRs show correct check status
- Add statuses: write permission for the new report-status job

This ensures fork PRs don't show failing checks from the initial
pull_request event, and the status from /ok-to-test runs is properly
reported back to the PR.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(e2e): increase scale-down timeout and remove backticks from comment

  to reduce flakiness in Kind emulated environment

fix(e2e): use correct saturation ConfigMap name matching Helm deployment

The e2e tests were using the hardcoded name "saturation-scaling-config" but
the Helm chart creates the ConfigMap with prefix "workload-variant-autoscaler-".
Updated the constant to use the correct name and removed the shadowing local

  with proper error handling for unsupported architectures

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add /ok-to-test gate for fork PRs and re-enable test-e2e

## /ok-to-test Gate for Fork PRs

External contributors opening PRs from forks now see a helpful message
instead of a failing CI run. The workflow:

1. For maintainers (write access): Tests run automatically
2. For external contributors: Posts instructions and skips tests
3. Maintainers can comment `/ok-to-test` to trigger tests after review

This uses the `issue_comment` event which runs in base repo context,
giving access to secrets and self-hosted runners after maintainer
approval.

Security model:
- External contributor opens PR (untrusted code)
- Maintainer reviews the code manually
- Maintainer comments `/ok-to-test` (explicit approval)
- Workflow runs with secrets access

## Re-enable test-e2e in CI

Added Kind installation and `make test-e2e` to ci-pr-checks.yaml
to run emulated e2e tests on every PR.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(e2e): multi-model testing with 2 models in 2 namespaces, 1 shared WVA

Multi-model E2E Testing:
- Deploy 2 models in 2 namespaces with 1 shared WVA controller
- Model A in llm-d-inference-scheduler-pr-XXX
- Model B in llm-d-inference-scheduler-pr-XXX-b
- Shared WVA in llm-d-autoscaler-pr-XXX

Test Improvements:
- Move HPA retrieval before VA stabilization to know minReplicas
- Wait for VA to stabilize at exact minReplicas before load test
- Increase stabilization timeout to 5 minutes
- Route load through Istio gateway instead of direct vLLM service

CI Cleanup Behavior:
- Before tests: Clean up all PR namespaces for fresh start
- After successful tests: Clean up automatically
- After failed tests: Leave resources for debugging

Documentation:
- Add monitoring commands with PR number placeholder
- Document multi-model testing architecture
- Document CI cleanup behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Recompile all gh-aw workflows with v0.33.12

Updates awf from v0.6.0 to v0.7.0 which fixes the EACCES permission
error when spawning shell commands. The new version uses direct binary
execution instead of npx, avoiding the shell spawn issue.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ci): auto-trigger /ok-to-test for maintainer fork PRs

Fork PRs don't have access to secrets on the pull_request event,
even when the author is a maintainer/admin. This caused e2e tests
to be skipped silently for maintainer fork PRs.

Changes:
- For maintainer/admin fork PRs: attempt to auto-post /ok-to-test
  comment to trigger issue_comment workflow
- For external contributors: attempt to post instructions to wait
  for maintainer approval
- Added error handling for comment posting since GitHub restricts
  fork PRs from posting comments on pull_request events
- Updated contributor message to be clearer

Note: Due to GitHub security restrictions, fork PRs cannot post
comments on pull_request events. Maintainers will need to manually
comment /ok-to-test on their own fork PRs.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(e2e): use >= assertion for VA stabilization check

The e2e test was failing intermittently because it expected the VA's
DesiredOptimizedAlloc.NumReplicas to equal exactly minReplicas (1).
However, during controller startup, this value starts at 0 before the
saturation engine computes the first optimized allocation.

Change the assertion from Equal(hpaMinReplicas) to
BeNumerically(">=", hpaMinReplicas) to allow the test to pass once
the engine has initialized, regardless of whether the value is exactly
at minReplicas or higher due to residual load.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): add gateway restart and improved health check

- Add gateway restart step after InferencePool configuration to ensure
  Istio picks up the correct ext_proc configuration (fixes 502 errors)
- Improve health check to verify actual model data in response, not
  just HTTP 200 status
- Add load gen job log capture when arrival rate stays at 0 for
  debugging purposes
